Definition.text: Melee skills are attack skills used with melee weapons or the unarmed state and have the Melee gem tag.

Purpose.text: Identify skills that are delivered in melee range and that follow the Melee keyword path.

Core Rules.text: All melee skills so far are Attack skills; Melee subsets include Strike and Slam. Some melee skills are not Strike/Slam but still carry Melee (for example Blade Flurry or Cyclone).

Internal Rules.text: Melee is a skill keyword applied to skill data and skill tags; it gates which support/interaction rules that explicitly mention melee may apply.

Calculation Order.text: Keyword tagging is resolved during skill selection and scaling. If a support requires Melee, the support eligibility check is applied before final attack-hit resolution.

Priority Rules.text: Melee-specific support restrictions take precedence over generic ranges when both are evaluated for one skill slot.

Preconditions.text: The action must be a skill with the Melee skill tag and must be an Attack for the primary relationship.

Postconditions.text: The skill remains classified under Melee and can receive Melee-scope mechanics, limitations, and supports.

Limitations.text: A Melee skill can still be in other skill-tag categories (for example AoE/Movement/Channelling), and those systems must still be processed according to their own rules.

Caps.text: No Melee-level percentage cap is defined on the Melee keyword alone.

Formula.text: No standalone Melee formula; Melee is a keyword tag, not an arithmetic operator.

cannot_affect: spell delivery rules without explicit Melee interaction

Conflicts.text: Ranged skill assumptions do not apply when a skill is explicitly Melee.

Exceptions.text: Some skills with Melee tag are not Strike or Slam, so classification is tag-based, not subset-based only.

Hidden Rules.text: Melee is a mechanic label that may be inherited by gems and modified by weapon restrictions and support tables.

Developer Notes.text: Store Melee as a source-of-eligibility term. Keep Melee distinct from Attack and from nearby/area mechanics.

Historical Changes.text: No patch-level deltas were provided for this pass in this source snapshot.

text: Blade Flurry is tagged Melee while not being Strike or Slam.
All melee skills so far are Attack skills.
Melee is not equivalent to any Attack with an AoE area visual only.
Melee is not identical to using a bow or other projectile-only delivery.
